role_delineation_check: The Social Worker manages the clinical discharge plan and home health agency coordination, while the Patient Navigator focuses on auditing demographic data in Atlantis, educating the family on the Healing Partners program, and flagging caregiver anxiety to the clinical team.
narrative_summary: Arthur Miller is a 75-year-old gentleman recovering from cardiac bypass surgery who is eager to return home but faces a significant hurdle due to his wife’s mounting fear regarding his post-operative care. Although the clinical team has cleared him for discharge, his wife, Martha, expressed deep concern that she has not yet been taught how to manage his surgical dressings or his complex new medication regimen. The Navigator acted as a vital liaison by documenting this specific training gap in Atlantis and alerting the Social Worker to ensure the family received the necessary bedside instruction before leaving the facility.
demographic_audit_note: I performed a side-by-side audit of the facility face sheet and the Atlantis record; I corrected a typo in the home address and added a secondary contact number for the patient's daughter to ensure the home team can reach the family.
v_card_flyer_status: During a bedside visit, I helped Arthur's wife save the Healing Partners V-Card into her mobile phone to ensure she recognizes our caller ID and provided a physical flyer detailing what to expect during the first twenty-four hours back at home.
pre_dc_pulse_call: I conducted the twenty-four-hour pulse call with Martha, who confirmed they were ready for the transition now that the facility staff had completed the wound care demonstration; she expressed relief knowing the Medical Assistant would visit the following day.
atlantis_final_sync: After the Social Worker verbally confirmed that the home health agency had accepted the case and set a start date, I recorded the confirmed discharge date in Atlantis and scheduled the Medical Assistant for the first home visit.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The Navigator met with Arthur and his wife to introduce the program; Martha shared that she feels completely unprepared to manage his chest incision at home, which the Navigator noted as a significant concern.

The Navigator asked the Social Worker for an update on the transition timeline; the Social Worker noted that while a home health agency was being sought, the discharge could not proceed until Martha completed her bedside training.
The Navigator documented the family's anxiety in Atlantis and formally alerted the Social Worker to the specific need for a wound care demonstration for the spouse.

The Social Worker called the Navigator to confirm that the training was completed and that an agency had accepted the referral for a Tuesday discharge; the Navigator updated the transition log in Atlantis accordingly.

format_2_triples.situation: The patient's wife expressed high anxiety regarding the management of surgical dressings at home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Documented the family's sentiment in Atlantis and alerted the Social Worker to the need for caregiver training.

format_2_triples.situation: The primary contact number in the system was missing the final digit.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Updated the demographic profile in Atlantis after verifying the correct number on the facility face sheet.

format_2_triples.situation: The family was confused about the difference between the home health nurse and our transition support.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated the family on the Healing Partners program and the specific role of the Medical Assistant during the first visit.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: When Martha asked for a specific time for the home health nurse's arrival, the Navigator explained that she was waiting for the Social Worker to provide that update and documented the inquiry in Atlantis.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the boundary that the Social Worker owns the relationship with the home health agency and prevents the Navigator from providing unverified clinical schedules.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator met with the Social Worker to verbally confirm that the home health agency had accepted the case before proceeding with the Medical Assistant's schedule in Atlantis.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This follows the requirement to wait for verbal confirmation of agency acceptance from the Social Worker before scheduling the first visit, ensuring a coordinated transition.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: The Navigator noticed the family's confusion about cardiac medications and provided a detailed review of the new prescriptions to help ease Martha's anxiety about the discharge.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This is an overstep because the Navigator is prohibited from managing medications or providing clinical education, which is the sole responsibility of the facility's clinical team and the Social Worker.
